title: Athyrium rupicola
taxonomy: Athyrium rupicola is classified as a species of fern within the family Athyriaceae. This plant is part of the genus Athyrium, which comprises several species of ferns. The genus Athyrium is further classified within the subfamily Asplenoideae, which includes other related species of ferns. The subfamily Asplenoideae is part of the family Athyriaceae, which is a small family of ferns. The Athyrium rupicola is native to the mountainous regions of East Asia.
uses: Athyrium rupicola is a species of fern native to East Asia, but information on its medicinal uses is limited. intro: Athyrium rupicola, also known as rock lady fern, is a species of fern native to the mountainous regions of China and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Athyrium rupicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine and is often prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties.
